perturbation.original_text: 3.4 ACCEPTANCE OF MILESTONE DELIVERABLE; CHANGE REQUESTS Client will notify Developer in writing of any failure of a Milestone Deliverable to comply with the Specifications, or of any other objections, corrections, changes or amendments required (a Change Request), within ten (10) days of such Milestone's Acceptance Test Date. Any Change Request shall be sufficient to identify, with clarity, any objection, correction, change or amendment to such Milestone Deliverable. In the absence of a Change Request from Client within the time periods detailed herein, the Milestone Deliverable will be deemed accepted by Client.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.4 ACCEPTANCE OF MILESTONE DELIVERABLE; CHANGE REQUESTS Client will notify Developer in writing of any failure of a Milestone Deliverable to comply with the Specifications, or of any other objections, corrections, changes or amendments required within a reasonable time of such Milestone's Acceptance Test Date. Any Change Request shall be sufficient to identify, with clarity, any objection, correction, change or amendment to such Milestone Deliverable. In the absence of a Change Request from Client within the time periods detailed herein, the Milestone Deliverable will be deemed accepted by Client.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase '(a Change Request)' and replacing 'within ten (10) days' with 'within a reasonable time', the clause becomes vague and unenforceable. The original text specified a clear timeframe for submitting a Change Request, and defined a 'Change Request'. The modified text removes that concrete definition of a Change Request. It replaces the original specified term with the new one that is ambigious. Now, the agreement still talks about Change Requests but what constitues it is undefined. This now contradicts the definition of 'Change Request' and timelines for it, since if the client provides one, it is not known if it will be valid. This uncertainty is reffered to in 3.5, 3.7 and 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.5 REJECTION OF MILESTONE DELIVERABLE If any Milestone Deliverable does not satisfy the Client's Acceptance Test and Client provides Developer a Change Request concerning the same, Developer will have twenty (20) days from the receipt of such Change Request to correct the deficiencies, errors, corrections, modifications, bug- fixes or changes to the Deliverables as identified in the Change Request. Upon Client's receipt of a Milestone Deliverable following Developer's modification pursuant to Change Request (a Modified Deliverable), Client will have five (5) days to inspect, test and reevaluate such Modified Deliverable to determine acceptance. If Client does not notify Developer of any further failures, objections, changes, defects, or bugs in such Modified Deliverable, the Modified Deliverable will be deemed accepted by Client.
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the specific 'ten (10) days' timeframe for submitting a Change Request and replaces it with 'within a reasonable time'. This introduces ambiguity and contradicts the requirement in clause 3.5 for the Client to provide a 'Change Request' concerning any failure to satisfy the Acceptance Test. Without a clear definition and timeframe, the validity and enforceability of a Change Request become uncertain, directly conflicting with the assumption in 3.5 that a Change Request is a well-defined and timely document.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Change Orders Sometimes during the term of this Agreement change order may or may not be requested by Client. However, If Client requests that Developer provide any additional Services or Deliverables or functionalities beyond those detail d in an applicable SOW, or requests a modification or change to any of the Services or Deliverables if possible, client will: (A) Submit to Developer, by means of a written order, all requests r additional services that alter, amend, enhance, add to, or delete any of the Services or Deliverables (a Change Order); (B) Developer will evaluate each Change Order, and within five (5) days of its receipt, will provide Client with (i) the change in Fee costs as a result of the Change Order, (ii) the impact, if any, of the Change Order on an aspect of the Delivery Schedule including any Milestone Date, Acceptance Test Date, or the Final Delivery Date, and (iii) the availability of Developer' resources to carry out the additional requested services detailed in the Change Order; (C) If Developer agrees to carry out the proposed Change Order, the Parties will execute an amended SOW or Change Order reflecting the Service and Deliverable changes; (D) Upon duly executing an amended SOW or executed Change Ord r, Developer will begin performance in accordance with the same. Developer has no obligation to perform any additional services before receiving the duly- executed amended SOW or executed Change Order, and Client has no obligation to pay Developer any Fees for services performed pursuant to an amended SOW or Change Order before the same; and (E) Once fully completed and executed, each amended SOW or Change Order will be deemed to be incorporated into and be part of this Agreement and will constitute a formal amendment to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Change Orders Sometimes during the term of this Agreement change order may or may not be requested by Client. However, If Client requests that Developer provide any additional Services or Deliverables or functionalities beyond those detail d in an applicable SOW, or requests a modification or change to any of the Services or Deliverables if possible, client will: (A) Submit to Developer, by means of a written order, all requests r additional services that alter, amend, enhance, add to, or delete any of the Services or Deliverables; (B) Developer will evaluate each request, and within five (5) days of its receipt, will provide Client with (i) the change in Fee costs as a result of the request, (ii) the impact, if any, of the request on an aspect of the Delivery Schedule including any Milestone Date, Acceptance Test Date, or the Final Delivery Date, and (iii) the availability of Developer' resources to carry out the additional requested services detailed in the request; (C) If Developer agrees to carry out the proposed change, the Parties will execute an amended SOW reflecting the Service and Deliverable changes; (D) Upon duly executing an amended SOW, Developer will begin performance in accordance with the same. Developer has no obligation to perform any additional services before receiving the duly- executed amended SOW, and Client has no obligation to pay Developer any Fees for services performed pursuant to an amended SOW before the same; and (E) Once fully completed and executed, each amended SOW will be deemed to be incorporated into and be part of this Agreement and will constitute a formal amendment to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase '(a Change Order)' from Section 4(A) and 'or Change Order' from Sections 4(C), (D), and (E), the contract omits a crucial definition and formal process. While the text continues to discuss 'Change Orders', it no longer defines what constitutes one or how it is formally processed beyond a written order. This contradicts the intent of formalizing changes to the original scope of work, now making the implementation of changes ambiguous. This contradiction affects 3.4, 3.5, 3.7 where the term Change Request (Order) is been reffered to. A Change Order is no longer defined explicitly, creating uncertainty in how modifications to the project are managed and approved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.7 FINAL DELIVERABLE AND SUBJECT PROGRAM TESTING Notwithstanding anything contained herein, upon completion of the Final Deliverable set out in the Specifications thereto, Client will perform Acceptance Test on the Subject Program within fifteen (15) calendar days from the Acceptance Test Date in order to determine whether the Subject Program satisfies the acceptance criteria and operates with internal consistency. If the completed Subject Program does not satisfy the Client's Acceptance Tests and Client provides Developer a Change Request concerning the same, Developer will have fifteen (15) calendar days from the receipt of such Change Request to correct the deficiencies errors, corrections, modifications, bug-fixes or changes. Client shall then have ten (10) additional days to inspect, test and reevaluate the completed and modified Subject Program or Final Deliverable. If the Subject Program or Final Deliverable still does not satisfy the Client's acceptance criteria and/or the Acceptance Tests, Client shall have the option of either: (a) repeating the procedure set forth above; or (b) terminating this Agreement pursuant to Section 12 of this Agreement . If the Client does not notify the Developer of any further failures, objections, changes, or other defects, or bugs of or in the Subject Program via a Change Request, Client will be deemed to have accepted the Subject Program.
perturbation.justification: The omission of '(a Change Order)' and 'or Change Order' from Section 4 of the contract significantly weakens the formal process for implementing changes. This alteration creates a direct contradiction with Section 3.7, which explicitly refers to and relies on 'Change Requests' for addressing deficiencies in the Final Deliverable. Without a clear definition and formalized process for 'Change Orders' as outlined in the original Section 4, the reference to 'Change Requests' in Section 3.7 becomes ambiguous and unenforceable, as the mechanism for creating and processing these requests is no longer explicitly defined. The omission directly breaks compatibility between the sections, impacting clarity and enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 10.3 NON DISPARAGEMENT CLAUSE The Client and the Designer both agree to never to disparage or speak ill of the other party to anyone and or post negative or disparaging comments Online regarding any of the Designer's products, services, affiliates, subsidiaries, officers, directors, employees or shareholders, and will take reasonable steps to prevent and will not knowingly permit any of their respective employees or agents to, disparage or speak ill of such persons. For purposes of this Section, "disparage" shall mean any negative statement whether written or oral, about Seattle Software Developers, Inc., Dot Com LLC. and or any of its affiliates. The Client and Designer both agree not to post on defamatory websites or review websites any negative posts concerning each other, the names of our companies, and our employees. Both the Client and the Designer both agree and acknowledge that this non-disparagement provision is a material term of this Agreement, the absence of which would have resulted in the Company refusing to enter into this Agreement. Subject to the terms, conditions, express representations and warranties provided in this Agreement, Designer and Client both agree to indemnify, save and hold armless each other from any and all damages, liabilities, costs, losses or expenses arising out of any finding of fact which is inconsistent with Designer's representations and warranties made herein, except in the event any such claims, damages, liabilities, costs, losses or expenses arise directly as a result of gross negligence or misconduct of Client.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.3 NON DISPARAGEMENT CLAUSE The Client and the Designer both agree to never to disparage or speak ill of the other party to anyone and or post negative or disparaging comments Online regarding any of the Designer's products, services, affiliates, subsidiaries, officers, directors, employees or shareholders, and will take reasonable steps to prevent and will not knowingly permit any of their respective employees or agents to, disparage or speak ill of such persons. The Client and Designer both agree not to post on defamatory websites or review websites any negative posts concerning each other, the names of our companies, and our employees. Both the Client and the Designer both agree and acknowledge that this non-disparagement provision is a material term of this Agreement, the absence of which would have resulted in the Company refusing to enter into this Agreement. Subject to the terms, conditions, express representations and warranties provided in this Agreement, Designer and Client both agree to indemnify, save and hold armless each other from any and all damages, liabilities, costs, losses or expenses arising out of any finding of fact which is inconsistent with Designer's representations and warranties made herein, except in the event any such claims, damages, liabilities, costs, losses or expenses arise directly as a result of gross negligence or misconduct of Client.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the definition of 'disparage' within the non-disparagement clause, the provision becomes significantly weaker and more difficult to enforce. The original text defined 'disparage' as any negative statement, written or oral, about specific entities. Removing this definition creates ambiguity about what actions constitute disparagement. This is reffered to in Section 12.1 where disputes might occur, but without the definition to follow, it might be hard to make a claim.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.1 CHOICE OF LAW AND VENUE This Agreement will be governed by the laws of the State of Washington without regards for its conflict of laws principle. The Parties will conduct friendly negotiations to resolve any dispute arising from this Agreement, including mediation if requested by either Party. Should mediation fail, each party consents to the personal jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in King County, Washington. If there is a dispute between the Parties relating to this Agreement, the Party substantially prevailing will be entitled to recover all costs and expenses of any subsequent proceeding (including trial, appellate, and arbitration proceedings), including reasonable attorneys' fees and costs incurred therein.
perturbation.justification: By removing the definition of 'disparage' from Section 10.3, the modified text introduces ambiguity regarding what constitutes disparagement. This directly contradicts Section 12.1, which anticipates disputes arising from the agreement. Without a clear definition of 'disparage,' resolving disputes related to the non-disparagement clause becomes significantly more challenging and may render the clause unenforceable, creating a strong contradiction as it makes enforcing the non-disparagement provision difficult in the event of a dispute.
